evals.prompt: update my pr description, the repo has a template in .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with Summary, Test Plan, and Breaking Changes sections. diff is 4 files adding jwt auth to src/auth/
evals.expected_output: Reads the PR template and generates a title (conventional commit format, under 72 chars) plus body that follows the template's section structure (Summary, Test Plan, Breaking Changes). Shows preview, asks to confirm, then runs gh pr edit. Never pushes.
expectations: Uses the repo's PR template structure (Summary, Test Plan, Breaking Changes sections) rather than a generic format
Generates a conventional commit-style title under 72 characters
Does NOT run git push, gt submit, or any push command — only updates the PR description via gh pr edit

evals.prompt: /pr-descr — i'm on main, no feature branch
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the user is on main with no associated PR. Flags this as an edge case ('Did you mean to be on a feature branch?') and stops. Does not attempt to create a PR or generate a description for a nonexistent PR.
expectations: Recognizes the user is on main and flags it as an edge case
Does NOT attempt to create a PR for main or generate a description for a nonexistent PR
Stops after flagging the issue rather than proceeding with the workflow

evals.prompt: /pr-descr --auto, no template in the repo, 2 commits adding a caching layer to the api
evals.expected_output: No template found, so generates title + body using fallback format (1-3 sentences explaining why with high-level how). In --auto mode, updates the PR directly via gh pr edit without asking for confirmation. Shows the PR URL when done.
expectations: Falls back to concise format (not a template) when no PR template exists
Updates directly without asking for confirmation in --auto mode
Shows the PR URL after updating
